posts.author: D1D4K10
posts.body: @GSPGOAT123 that’s why they are doing Van vs Taira for the title and not Van vs Kape who deserves it more

posts.author: The Giraffe
posts.body: Manel Kape has also missed weight a couple times, had to pull out from injury, and was pulled from a fight for testing positive for Turinabol metabolites, while Taira's record on that sort of stuff is clean. Taira being more reliable is a big factor.

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: Predictions: 5 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 905 others They’re both great fights so I’m fine with it either way. Right now I think Taira beats Van and Van beats Kape.Also great points @Giraffe * Edited at 02.16.2026, 11:13 AM ET *

posts.author: D1D4K10
posts.body: @The Giraffe I’m not denying what you said (as a big Kape fan) but Kape has a better recent resume. Kape is on a 3 fight W streak, Taira on a 2 fight W. Yes, Park was in short notice but even if Taira beat Albazi it would be a 2 fight win streak. All of their wins are by finish (Almabayev is debatable) but Kape deserves it “We make what the fans want to see” is not the UFC’s motto anymore. Who tf wanted to see Gaethje vs Pimblett for the interim or Volk vs Lopes rematch? Undeserved interim and undeserved title shot. Van vs Taira applies to this situation, I understand what giraffe said but arguably Dana wants an asian champion to grow their audience. @Great (Again, without bias) Imo Kape beats both of them and Taira beats Van. I do not like Josh Van at all but both of them would beat him anyways * Edited at 02.16.2026, 1:24 PM ET *

posts.author: Mazeflare
posts.body: @D1D4K10 I understand your frustration completely, I share your sentiment for the most part, but as frustrated fan with the modern days UFC matchmaking, I'm definitelly fine with Taira getting the shot (not because I'm a big Taira fan, won't deny) but because Taira is developing his game on a incredibly high-rate to the point that even I counted him out on his last fight and the element of new generation versus new generation intrigues even more.Gaethje vs Paddy? Atrocious.Volk vs Lopes 2? Revolting.Van vs Taira, in my humble opinion, right timing and amazing.If you give the shot to Kape, there will be always uncertainty whether he will make it to the fight or not, plus, we would the boat on this incredible match-up with Van vs Taira. The UFC missed the boat with Jon Jones vs Anthony Johnson (R.I.P.), Jon Jones vs Francis Ngannou, Jon Jones vs Tom Aspinall, and low-key, Makhachev vs Topuria (at 170 it will not be competitive), this is an opportunity that shouldn't be missed. * Edited at 02.16.2026, 2:10 PM ET *

posts.author: A_Person_05
posts.body: Predictions: 13 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 905 others @B7L7 And Yadong's striking looked bad. To be clear after watching that fight the conclusion I came to was that O'Malley and Yadong both looked terrible. Cory smokes both of them, Yan smokes either of them, Umar and Merab smoke both of them. Song specifically has looked like lost everything that made him a prospect on the rise over time. He rarely ever lands power shots with his hands anymore. He can't throw in combination. He went out there and tried to leg kick and clinch his way to a win. It was a bad performace.

posts.author: madroachin
posts.body: Predictions: 12 of 13 Pending | Tied for 1st with 905 others Song vs. FigueiredoYadong Song, DECThis will be a tough fight for Song, but he will be able to survive the danger of Figueiredo early and solidify some rounds in his favor to get the nod. This fight favors Figueiredo the longer it goesZhang vs. MenifieldMingyang Zhang, TKO, R2Zhang will be able to clip Menifield and get him out of therePavlovich vs. TeixeiraSergei Pavlovich, TKO, R1Pavlovich way too strong, hopefully he comes in to finish unlike his latest performancesPerez vs. SumudaerjiSumudaerji, DECSumudaerji has the chin to outlast, this is a theme for the card. (Also genuinely surprised they re-signed Perez)Asakura vs. SmothermanCameron Smotherman, DECKai won't get subbed, but Smotherman will edge out the decision through the grapplingSalikhov vs. MatthewsJake Matthews, SUB, R3Matthews has the chin to withstand, before getting a submission on a tired SalikhovHill vs. XiongJingnan Xiong, DECXiong split decision...Dias vs. LeeLuis Felipe Dias, SUB, R2Dias will make this look easy. I expect a short grappling match after Dias catches Lee on the feet.Tsuruya vs. AguilarRei Tsuruya, DECIf Tsuruya has improved in the striking, he will comfortably take the decision by outclassing on the ground and on the feet. Aguilar would be the champion at 115lbsLookboonmee vs. AmorimLoma Lookboonmee, DECI rate Lookboonmee's competition higher than Amorim's. Lookboonmee will stuff the takedowns and edge out a decision.Aoriqileng vs. HaddonCody Haddon, DECHaddon is more consistent and beats Aoriqileng on the groundDing vs. HenriqueMeng Ding, TKO, R2Betting Ding finds the shot over Henrique being perfect for 3 rounds.
